00030 /*
00031  * Main.
00032  */
00033 
00034 #include <types.h>
00035 #include <kern/errno.h>
00036 #include <kern/reboot.h>
00037 #include <kern/unistd.h>
00038 #include <lib.h>
00039 #include <spl.h>
00040 #include <clock.h>
00041 #include <thread.h>
00042 #include <current.h>
00043 #include <synch.h>
00044 #include <vm.h>
00045 #include <mainbus.h>
00046 #include <vfs.h>
00047 #include <device.h>
00048 #include <syscall.h>
00049 #include <test.h>
00050 #include <version.h>
00051 #include <kern/file.h>
00052 #include "autoconf.h"  // for pseudoconfig
00053 
00054 
00055 /*
00056  * These two pieces of data are maintained by the makefiles and build system.
00057  * buildconfig is the name of the config file the kernel was configured with.
00058  * buildversion starts at 1 and is incremented every time you link a kernel. 
00059  *
00060  * The purpose is not to show off how many kernels you've linked, but
00061  * to make it easy to make sure that the kernel you just booted is the
00062  * same one you just built.
00063  */
00064 extern const int buildversion;
00065 extern const char buildconfig[];
00066 extern void hello(void);
00067 extern bool con_attached;
00068 /*
00069  * Copyright message for the OS/161 base code.
00070  */
00071 static const char harvard_copyright[] =
00072     "Copyright (c) 2000, 2001, 2002, 2003, 2004, 2005, 2008, 2009\n"
00073     "   President and Fellows of Harvard College.  All rights reserved.\n";
00074 
00075 
00076 /*
00077  * Initial boot sequence.
00078  */
00079 static
00080 void
00081 boot(void)
00082 {
00083         /*
00084          * The order of these is important!
00085          * Don't go changing it without thinking about the consequences.
00086          *
00087          * Among other things, be aware that console output gets
00088          * buffered up at first and does not actually appear until
00089          * mainbus_bootstrap() attaches the console device. This can
00090          * be remarkably confusing if a bug occurs at this point. So
00091          * don't put new code before mainbus_bootstrap if you don't
00092          * absolutely have to.
00093          *
00094          * Also note that the buffer for this is only 1k. If you
00095          * overflow it, the system will crash without printing
00096          * anything at all. You can make it larger though (it's in
00097          * dev/generic/console.c).
00098          */
00099 
00100         kprintf("\n");
00101         kprintf("OS/161 base system version %s\n", BASE_VERSION);
00102         kprintf("%s", harvard_copyright);
00103         kprintf("\n");
00104         
00105         hello(); /* Print a welcome message */
00106         
00107         kprintf("System version %s (%s #%d) \n", GROUP_VERSION, buildconfig, buildversion);
00108         
00109         kprintf("\n");
00110         
00111         con_attached = false;
00112 
00113         /* Early initialization. */
00114         ram_bootstrap();
00115         thread_bootstrap();
00116         hardclock_bootstrap();
00117         vfs_bootstrap();
00118 
00119         /* Probe and initialize devices. Interrupts should come on. */
00120         kprintf("Device probe...\n");
00121         KASSERT(curthread->t_curspl > 0);
00122         mainbus_bootstrap();
00123         KASSERT(curthread->t_curspl == 0);
00124         /* Now do pseudo-devices. */
00125         pseudoconfig();
00126         kprintf("\n");
00127 
00128         con_attached = true;
00129 
00130         /* Late phase of initialization. */
00131         vm_bootstrap();
00132         kprintf_bootstrap();
00133         thread_start_cpus();
00134 
00135         /* Default bootfs - but ignore failure, in case emu0 doesn't exist */
00136         vfs_setbootfs("emu0");
00137 
00138 
00139         /*
00140          * Make sure various things aren't screwed up.
00141          */
00142         COMPILE_ASSERT(sizeof(userptr_t) == sizeof(char *));
00143         COMPILE_ASSERT(sizeof(*(userptr_t)0) == sizeof(char));
00144 }
00145 
00146 /*
00147  * Shutdown sequence. Opposite to boot().
00148  */
00149 static
00150 void
00151 shutdown(void)
00152 {
00153 
00154         kprintf("Shutting down.\n");
00155         
00156         vfs_clearbootfs();
00157         vfs_clearcurdir();
00158         vfs_unmountall();
00159 
00160         thread_shutdown();
00161 
00162         splhigh();
00163 }
00164 
00165 /*****************************************/
00166 
00167 /*
00168  * reboot() system call.
00169  *
00170  * Note: this is here because it's directly related to the code above,
00171  * not because this is where system call code should go. Other syscall
00172  * code should probably live in the "syscall" directory.
00173  */
00174 int
00175 sys_reboot(int code)
00176 {
00177         switch (code) {
00178             case RB_REBOOT:
00179             case RB_HALT:
00180             case RB_POWEROFF:
00181                 break;
00182             default:
00183                 return EINVAL;
00184         }
00185 
00186         shutdown();
00187 
00188         switch (code) {
00189             case RB_HALT:
00190                 kprintf("The system is halted.\n");
00191                 mainbus_halt();
00192                 break;
00193             case RB_REBOOT:
00194                 kprintf("Rebooting...\n");
00195                 mainbus_reboot();
00196                 break;
00197             case RB_POWEROFF:
00198                 kprintf("The system is halted.\n");
00199                 mainbus_poweroff();
00200                 break;
00201         }
00202 
00203         panic("reboot operation failed\n");
00204         return 0;
00205 }
00206 
00207 /*
00208  * Kernel main. Boot up, then fork the menu thread; wait for a reboot
00209  * request, and then shut down.
00210  */
00211 void
00212 kmain(char *arguments)
00213 {
00214         boot();
00215 
00216         menu(arguments);
00217 
00218         /* Should not get here */
00219 }
Generated on Sun Jun 27 22:12:13 2010 for Os/161 by  1.6.1
